summary: Partially decompiled the common fighter BuryWait and BuryJump action routines by removing their assembly from asm/melee/ft/ft_0C08.s and adding C implementations in ftCo_Bury.c. The file report shows the newly covered BuryWait/BuryJump routines at 100% for ftCo_Bury.c, while the overall file remained partially unmatched because other bury functions still had nonzero scores. The PR also tightened related types and prototypes, including introducing ftCommon_BuryType use in ftCo_800C0C88, adding a buryjump motion-var field, expanding ftCommonData fields around x618-x620, and correcting ft_80082D40 to take one float argument.
what_changed: Deleted the assembly definitions for ftCo_BuryWait_Anim, ftCo_BuryWait_IASA, ftCo_BuryWait_Phys, ftCo_BuryWait_Coll, ftCo_800C13BC, ftCo_BuryJump_Anim, ftCo_BuryJump_IASA, ftCo_BuryJump_Phys, and ftCo_BuryJump_Coll from asm/melee/ft/ft_0C08.s.
Added C implementations for the BuryWait and BuryJump routines in src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Bury.c.

Implemented ftCo_BuryWait_Anim as a timer decrement on Fighter.x1A4C using p_ftCommonData->x610, followed by ftCommon_8007DC08 with p_ftCommonData->x614, transitioning through ftCo_800C13BC when x1A4C reaches the zero constant ftCo_804D8C38.
Implemented ftCo_BuryWait_IASA and ftCo_BuryWait_Phys as empty functions matching the original blr-only assembly.
Represented ftCo_BuryWait_Coll as a C wrapper around ftCo_Bury_Coll in non-MUST_MATCH/WIP builds, with a TODO and dummy stack bytes under MUST_MATCH because the original emitted unused stack.

Implemented ftCo_800C13BC as the transition out of BuryWait into BuryJump or a related fallback: if ftCo_800C5240 succeeds it calls ftCo_800C5A98, otherwise it clears/sets fighter velocity and motion variables, calls Fighter_ChangeMotionState with ftCo_MS_BuryJump, calls ftCommon_8007E2F4, and updates collision via ftColl_8007B7A4.
Implemented ftCo_BuryJump_Anim to increment fp->mv.co.buryjump.x0 by ftCo_804D8C3C and call ftCo_800CC730 when ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining returns false.

Implemented ftCo_BuryJump_IASA as a gated input-cancel chain once the buryjump counter reaches p_ftCommonData->x61C, using RETURN_IF around ftCo_SpecialAir_CheckInput, ftCo_80095328, ftCo_800D7100, ftCo_800C3B10, ftCo_80099A58, ftCo_AttackAir_CheckItemThrowInput, ftCo_800D705C, and ftCo_800CB870.
Implemented ftCo_BuryJump_Phys using ftCommon_8007D494 with co_attrs.grav and co_attrs.terminal_vel, followed by ftCommon_8007D268, again documenting unused stack under MUST_MATCH.
Implemented ftCo_BuryJump_Coll to call ft_80082D40 when vertical self velocity is nonnegative and ftCo_AirCatchHit_Coll otherwise.
Changed ftCo_800C0C88 from taking enum_t and numeric switch cases to taking ftCommon_BuryType and using BuryType_Unk1, BuryType_Unk2, and BuryType_Unk3.
Moved BuryWait/BuryJump prototypes from ft_0C08.h into ftCo_Bury.h and changed ft_0C08.h's include guard from GALE01_0C12D8 to GALE01_0C08.
Added ftCommon_MotionVars.co.buryjump.x0 at fp+2340, matching the field used by BuryJump_Anim and ftCo_800C13BC.
Refined ftCommonData fields x618 and x61C from UNK_T to float, x620 to int, and shifted the following byte array to x624.
Changed ft_80082D40's declaration and asm definition from taking two float parameters to one float parameter; updated ftCo_StopCeil.c and BuryJump_Coll call sites accordingly.
Made ftCo_8009EB18 non-static in ftCo_BarrelWait.h, presumably to satisfy cross-file calls or linkage needed by the bury code, although no review discussion explains the reason.
Added new empty ftCo_BuryWait.c and minimal ftCo_BuryWait.h files; the diff does not show functional content there, so their intended future role is weakly evidenced.
smart_moves: Kept difficult-but-understood routines as C bodies guarded by ASM/MUST_MATCH fallbacks, allowing normal builds to use readable C while preserving exact assembly scaffolding where needed.
Collapsed ftCo_BuryWait_Coll into a call to the already matched ftCo_Bury_Coll in the C path rather than duplicating equivalent collision logic, while acknowledging the original stack mismatch.
Used RETURN_IF in ftCo_BuryJump_IASA to model the original chain of early-exit input checks compactly and in the same order as the assembly call sequence.
Introduced a dedicated buryjump motion variable field instead of continuing to reference raw fp+2340 storage, improving future readability and matching work.
Replaced magic numeric bury-type switch cases with ftCommon_BuryType enum constants, making ftCo_800C0C88 easier to reason about without changing behavior.
Corrected function signatures and common-data field types as matching evidence demanded, especially ft_80082D40 and p_ftCommonData fields around x618-x620.
Removed matched routines from the standalone asm file once C replacements existed, reducing the remaining asm surface in ft_0C08.s.
decomp_lessons: When decompiling common action-state callbacks, match the Anim/IASA/Phys/Coll quartet together because fields introduced by one routine, such as mv.co.buryjump.x0, are often initialized or consumed by the others.
Small blr-only callbacks should be represented as empty C functions; this PR shows ftCo_BuryWait_IASA and ftCo_BuryWait_Phys reaching 100% that way.
A collision callback may match semantically by delegating to an existing equivalent routine, but unused original stack can still require MUST_MATCH guards or TODO dummy locals.
Input-cancel routines often compile cleanly as an ordered early-return chain; preserve assembly order when translating calls to avoid subtle control-flow mismatches.
CommonData fields should be typed from observed use: lfs/stfs evidence in these routines supported float x618/x61C, while x620 was passed as an integer-like collision parameter.
Prototype fixes can be part of matching: correcting ft_80082D40 to one float was necessary both for the new BuryJump_Coll call and existing StopCeil call cleanup.
Header ownership matters: once ftCo_Bury.c owns these routines, the prototypes belong in ftCo_Bury.h rather than a broad address-range header like ft_0C08.h.

A partial PR can still land many 100% functions while leaving unrelated functions in the same file unmatched; the human report lists ftCo_BuryJump_IASA, ftCo_800C13BC, ftCo_BuryWait_Anim, ftCo_BuryWait_Coll, ftCo_BuryJump_Anim, ftCo_BuryJump_Phys, and ftCo_BuryJump_Coll at 100%.
naming_conventions: Common fighter action callbacks use the pattern ftCo_<State>_Anim, ftCo_<State>_IASA, ftCo_<State>_Phys, and ftCo_<State>_Coll, as shown for BuryWait and BuryJump.
Unknown common motion-variable subfields were named under semantic state scopes, e.g. fp->mv.co.buryjump.x0, rather than left as raw offsets.
Unknown enum values were still given enum-scoped names, BuryType_Unk1/BuryType_Unk2/BuryType_Unk3, which is better than raw numeric cases while preserving uncertainty.
Address-bearing unknown helpers retained ftCo_800C13BC-style names until behavior-specific names were not evidenced.
Header include guards follow GALE01_<address-range>; this PR corrected ft_0C08.h to GALE01_0C08 after removing 0C12D8-specific declarations.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed 235 lines of assembly from ft_0C08.s after replacing the BuryWait/BuryJump range with C.
Used ASM functions with `#if !defined(MUST_MATCH) || defined(WIP)` C bodies and inline assembly fallbacks for functions that still needed exact-match protection.
Inserted dummy `u8 _[8] = { 0 };` locals under MUST_MATCH for functions with unused stack noted by TODO comments.
Used `nofralloc` inline asm fallbacks and `#pragma peephole on` around preserved assembly regions.
Matched float comparisons against constants ftCo_804D8C38 and ftCo_804D8C3C to preserve the original zero/one-like behavior.
Translated original branch chains into compact early-return macros in ftCo_BuryJump_IASA rather than preserving verbose nested conditionals.
Aligned struct field additions with observed offsets: buryjump.x0 at fp+2340 and common-data floats at +618/+61C.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or formal reviews were present in the provided PR slice.

Because there is no reviewer discussion, inferred motivation for some header/linkage edits, such as making ftCo_8009EB18 non-static or adding empty ftCo_BuryWait files, is weak and should not be treated as documented reviewer intent.
follow_up_queries: Search later PRs for ftCo_BuryWait.c or ftCo_BuryWait.h to see whether the empty files were populated or removed.
Search later PRs for renames of ftCo_800C13BC, ftCo_800C5240, ftCo_800C5A98, and ftCo_800CB870 after BuryJump behavior became clearer.
Check whether ftCommonData x610, x614, x618, x61C, and x620 received semantic names in subsequent common-data struct expansion.
Verify whether the MUST_MATCH dummy stack TODOs in ftCo_BuryWait_Coll and ftCo_BuryJump_Phys were resolved by cleaner C ordering or compiler settings.
Search for additional ft_80082D40 call sites to confirm the one-float prototype remained stable after this PR.
